notes: Phase order reflects the recipe's variant chain — variant 1 (clean Ctrl-D exit) → variant 2 (SIGKILL mid-idle) → variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn). BACKFLOW (live recording 2026-06-12, two consecutive runs, structurally identical): of the three teardowns, ONLY the SIGKILL-mid-idle variant reliably emits process_exited in-window. gemini-cli's clean Ctrl-D exit (v1) does NOT emit process_exited — the node launcher exit races the next session's cwd-keyed PID rebind, so the daemon never observes the death; the session simply settles to ready (the 'State is ready at exit' observable, asserted as v1_turn_done). The SIGKILL-mid-turn variant (v3) is killed while working and the 8 s tail is too short for the 5 s process-scan sweep to fire before the recording daemon shuts down, so it ends in working with no process_exited either. Per the assess caveat ('if a variant leaves no trace, assert only the observable one') the spec asserts ONLY the real observable arc: v1 = clean exit settles to ready; v2 = SIGKILL-mid-idle → process_exited (the load-bearing teardown observable); v3 = mid-turn SIGKILL leaves the session in working with no settle. Each variant's birth is pinned new_session:true so a later variant can't match an earlier variant's ready. gemini-cli has no explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is inferred (assistant text + zero open toolCalls).

text: Variant 1: first user prompt drives ready → working

Variant 1: turn completes back to ready (inferred turn_done — assistant text, zero open toolCalls). The session settles to ready and the clean Ctrl-D exit leaves it there — gemini-cli emits NO process_exited for a clean exit (the launcher death races the next session's PID rebind), so 'State is ready at exit' is asserted as this settle, not as a process_exited.
Variant 2: new session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 2: turn completes back to ready (idle window begins)

Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires while the session is in ready; no transition to working or waiting between v2_turn_done and process_exited (satisfies 'state never entered working post-exit'). This is the ONE teardown gemini-cli reliably surfaces as process_exited.
Variant 3: new session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes

Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — user prompt drives ready → working, then the session is killed mid-turn. No process_exited is asserted: the kill lands while working and the 8 s tail is too short for the 5 s process-scan sweep before the recording daemon shuts down, so the recording ends with the session in working (the spec's 'State does not stick in working' is a live-dashboard property the sweep handles, outside the recording tail).
